title: FUSION Audit Event
description: Z-AUDIT event schema. OCSF-aligned structure, ECS-compatible field naming. See docs/decisions/0003-adopt-ocsf-audit-schema.md.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp with millisecond precision. Example: 2026-05-04T14:32:01.123Z

event_id.description: UUIDv7 — monotonically increasing, time-ordered. Globally unique per event.

action.description: verb.noun identifying the auditable action. Examples: deploy.solution, read.secret, authn.success.

class_uid.description: OCSF class identifier. Provides a stable machine-readable event class contract for SIEM detection rules.

id.description: OIDC subject claim (sub) of the entity that triggered the action.

type.description: user = human via OIDC; service = machine identity; agent = AI coding agent.

session_id.description: OIDC session ID when available. Enables session-level audit correlation.

type.description: Resource type the action was performed on.

id.description: Stable identifier of the subject resource.

name.description: Human-readable name of the subject at the time of the event. Denormalized for readability.

reason.description: Required when outcome is failure or denied. Human-readable explanation. MUST NOT contain secrets or PII.

request_id.description: Correlation ID propagated end-to-end via X-Request-ID header. Ties the audit event to application logs.

ip.description: IP address of the caller as seen by Z-API. May be a load balancer IP in proxied environments.

user_agent.description: HTTP User-Agent of the caller. Aids in distinguishing browser, CLI, and service clients.

classification.description: Data classification of the subject resource at the time of the action. Aligns with Z-AUDIT data classification policy.

git_sha.description: Full 40-character Git SHA of the artifact being deployed or verified. Required for deploy.* and signature.verify actions.

environment.description: FUSION stage environment. Enables filtering across multi-stage sink configurations.

schema_version.description: Semver of this schema. Consumers MUST reject events where the major version does not match their compiled expectation.

product.description: Identifies the emitting system. Constant for all FUSION audit events.

emit_version.description: Version of the audit emit library that produced this event. Aids in diagnosing schema drift.
